_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval.

procedure.name: Endoscopic ultrasound (EUS), upper GI, with FNA

code.display: Endoscopic ultrasonography of upper gastrointestinal tract (procedure)

procedure.indication: Characterization of an 18 mm subepithelial gastric lesion identified on outside EGD; asymptomatic
procedure.extent_reached: Second portion of duodenum; radial and linear echoendoscope examination of stomach completed

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Endoscopic ultrasound (EUS) of the upper GI tract with fine-needle aspiration/biopsy. INDICATION: 18 mm subepithelial gastric lesion found on outside EGD; asymptomatic. SEDATION: MAC (propofol). FINDINGS: The echoendoscope was advanced without difficulty. Endoscopically, a smooth subepithelial bulge with intact overlying mucosa was seen along the gastric body/fundus (greater curve), without ulceration. On radial and linear EUS, a well-circumscribed, hypoechoic, homogeneous lesion measuring 19 x 16 mm was identified arising from the fourth echolayer (muscularis propria). No internal cystic spaces, calcification, or exophytic irregular borders. No peri-gastric lymphadenopathy; no ascites. Doppler confirmed avascular needle path. EUS-guided fine-needle biopsy was performed with a 22-gauge core needle x3 passes under real-time guidance; adequate cellular cores obtained on-site macroscopic assessment. No immediate bleeding at the puncture site. The examined esophagus, remainder of stomach, and duodenum to the second portion were normal. IMPRESSION: 19 mm hypoechoic lesion arising from the muscularis propria, most consistent with a gastrointestinal stromal tumor (GIST); differential includes leiomyoma or schwannoma. EUS-FNB performed; histopathology and immunohistochemistry pending.

findings.finding: Hypoechoic homogeneous subepithelial lesion arising from muscularis propria (4th layer), consistent with GIST vs leiomyoma/schwannoma
Normal esophagus, remaining stomach, and duodenum to D2

disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort. Post-EUS/FNB precautions reviewed; advised to report abdominal pain, fever, hematemesis, or melena.

followup.recommendation: Review EUS-FNB histopathology and immunohistochemistry (CD117/DOG-1/S100/desmin); communicate result to patient. If GIST confirmed, obtain staging CT and refer to surgical oncology / MDT for resection decision based on size and mitotic index
If pathology non-diagnostic or benign (e.g., leiomyoma), establish EUS surveillance interval for the subepithelial lesion
Reconcile lesion size against outside EGD report once received (referral 18 mm vs EUS 19 mm vs patient-recalled ~2 cm)

planted_followup_items.description: EUS-FNB histopathology/IHC of gastric muscularis-propria lesion pending — GIST is the leading diagnosis; result determines whether staging and surgical referral are triggered
planted_followup_items.expected_action: review path + IHC, call patient with result, and if GIST confirmed initiate staging CT and surgical oncology/MDT referral

planted_followup_items.description: Conditional surgical oncology / MDT referral for resection if GIST confirmed on pathology
planted_followup_items.expected_action: place surgical oncology referral and obtain staging CT once GIST confirmed

planted_followup_items.description: If lesion proves benign or biopsy non-diagnostic, an EUS surveillance interval for the subepithelial lesion must be set
planted_followup_items.expected_action: document EUS follow-up interval based on final pathology

planted_followup_items.description: Outside EGD report/images still outstanding; lesion size discrepancy (18 mm referral vs 19 mm EUS vs ~2 cm patient recollection) to be reconciled
planted_followup_items.expected_action: obtain outside records and reconcile documented lesion size/location

planted_followup_items.description: No prescription anticoagulant/antiplatelet in use ("blood thinner" = OTC fish oil); no periprocedural hold required — documented for completeness
planted_followup_items.expected_action: confirm no anticoagulant hold needed; document OTC fish oil on the medication list, may continue per patient preference

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a bit scratchy and my belly is kind of bloated and burpy since the ultrasound. No fever, no throwing up.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-upper-endoscopy sore throat and insufflation bloating; no red-flag features
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and bloating/gas are expected after EUS and self-limited over 24-48h. Return precautions if fever, severe/worsening abdominal pain, vomiting blood, or black stools.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms without any complication signal; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: When will I hear back about the sample you took? I've been a little anxious waiting on the results.
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending EUS-FNB pathology timing
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: histopathology/IHC pending, expected back ~2026-08-11; GI team will call with the result and next steps once finalized.
contacts.rationale: Answerable directly from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending_pathology item.

contacts.verbatim: Is it okay for me to keep taking my fish oil capsules, or should I hold off after this procedure?
contacts.clinical_signal: Medication question about OTC omega-3 (patient's 'blood thinner'); no true anticoagulant in use
contacts.expected_action: Answer: fish oil is an OTC supplement, not a prescription anticoagulant; no periprocedural hold was required and she may continue it per preference. Document on the medication list.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the med-reconciliation item; the 'blood thinner' was clarified as OTC fish oil, so no hold or resumption timing applies.
